executor_handoff.system_instructions: Preserve existing patterns. Follow scope exactly. Do NOT widen scope. Use ONLY the chosen path below. Readonly elsewhere. Swift 6 strict concurrency. macOS14+. Use serena for edits, xcode-tools for build/test. Project conventions (styleguide.md / unit-testing.md) override any skill defaults. NOTE: serena file paths need the `Valistream/` workspace prefix (e.g. Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/...). xcode-tools ExecuteSnippet does NOT.

task_brief.objective: Fix two report bugs, both rooted in ONE cause: the playlist-id namespace split. BUG A — every playlist shows "1 refresh" / Summary "Refreshes: 1" even on multi-minute live runs (real snapshot index reached 35). refreshCount frozen at its init value. BUG B — media playlist blocks render heading + subtitle but NO property bullets and NO "### Timing" section. Only the master block shows fields. Fix = unify the playlist track identity on the ALIAS (presentationID), so the track dict, the refresh-increment, and the info-join all key on the same string.
task_brief.why:
`playlistTracks` is keyed by `"<role>-<index>"` (e.g. "variant-0"). EVERYTHING else — archive folders, snapshot labels, evidence paths, info emission, the refresh increment, and the report.md heading/legend — keys on `presentationID` = `aliasRegistry.alias(for: url)?.alias` (e.g. "1080p_avc1-mp4a"). The two strings differ for every media playlist; they coincide ONLY for master (alias == "master" == track key), which is why master alone renders correctly.
Consequence A: `incrementRefreshCount(presentationID)` does `playlistTracks[alias]?.refreshCount += 1` → key miss → optional-chain no-op → count never advances past the init value 1. Consequence B: report builder joins `infoByID[playlist.id]` where playlist.id == track key ("variant-0") but PlaylistInformation.playlistID == alias → join misses → `information == nil` → `reportHeaderFields(nil)` / `reportTimingFields(nil)` return []/nil → empty media block. (The OLD pre-04 report iterated the information array directly with no id join, so it masked this; the 04 redesign switched to an id join and exposed the latent mismatch.)
task_brief.repo_signals:
ROOT (the only outlier key): - Session/ValidationSession.swift:66 `var playlistTracks: [String: PlaylistTrack] = [:]` - Session/ValidationSession.swift:493-502 `trackPlaylist(_ id:...)` → `playlistTracks[id] = ...` - Session/ValidationSession.swift:504-507 `incrementRefreshCount(_ playlistID:)` → `playlistTracks[playlistID]?.refreshCount += 1` ; `sessionRefreshTotal += 1` (sum, unaffected).
ID ASSIGNMENT SITES (master-children loop + single-media else): - Session/ValidationSession.swift:199 master: `trackPlaylist("master", kind:.master,...,refreshCount:1)` (master alias resolves to "master" by scheme → already aligned; leave as-is, see guidance). - Session/ValidationSession.swift:218-241 master-children loop. Key lines: 230 `let playlistID = "\(reference.role.rawValue)-\(index)"` 231 `await archiveFetch(load.result, requestURL: reference.url, playlistID: playlistID)` 233 `trackPlaylist(playlistID, kind:.media, role:reference.role, url:reference.url, selected:true, refreshCount:1)` 234 `let presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: reference.url)?.alias ?? playlistID` 236-240 `emitPlaylistInformation(playlistID: presentationID, playlist: playlist, streamKind: loadedKind)` - Session/ValidationSession.swift:253-261 else (direct media): 253 `trackPlaylist("media", kind:.media, role:.variant, url:inputURL, selected:true, refreshCount:1)` 255 `let presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: inputURL)?.alias ?? "media"` 257-261 `emitPlaylistInformation(playlistID: presentationID, ...)`
WHY archiveFetch needs NO change (self-resolves): - Session/ValidationSession+Reporting.swift:12 `archiveFetch(_ result:, requestURL:, playlistID:)`. :46-48 recomputes `presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: requestURL)?.alias` and uses THAT for the folder. The passed `playlistID` is only a fallback (master sniff @:50). Proven by the real session: all archive dirs are alias-named (1080p_avc1-mp4a/, ... incl. _0 initial snapshot).
MONITOR side (already alias-keyed — this is the increment caller): - Session/ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift:34 `presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: candidate.url)?.alias ?? candidate.id` - Session/ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift:109 `incrementRefreshCount(presentationID)` - Session/PlaylistSelection.swift:21 `Candidate.id`; :69 `Candidate(id:"media",...)`; :73 `Candidate(id: "\(reference.role.rawValue)-\(index)", ..., alias: aliasFor(reference.url))`. candidate.id == the init-loop playlistID and candidate.url == reference.url ⇒ after the fix the init-loop track key and the monitor's incrementRefreshCount key are byte-identical in BOTH the aliased and (degenerate) no-alias case. No residual edge.
CONSUMERS of playlistTracks (only two reads — both want the alias): - Session/ValidationSession+Reporting.swift:99 `playlistTracks.map { id, track in PlaylistInfo(id:id,...) }` → PlaylistInfo.id becomes the alias → flows to report.md (join + subtitle) AND report.json. - Session/ValidationSession.swift:505 increment (the fix target).
REPORT BUILDER (already correct; do NOT edit — just understand the join it depends on): - Session/SessionReportBuilder.swift buildMarkdown: `infoByID = Dictionary(playlistInformation.map { ($0.playlistID, $0) }, ...)`, `playlistBlock(information: infoByID[playlist.id], ...)`, `refreshes = playlists.map(\.refreshCount).max() ?? 0`, subtitle "\(refreshCount) refresh[es]". - Output/PlaylistInfoFormatter.reportHeaderFields(for:)/reportTimingFields(for:) → []/nil on nil.
task_brief.scope: IN: - Make media playlist tracks key on the alias (presentationID), matching every other subsystem. Two edits in ValidationSession.swift (master-children loop + direct-media else). - Add ONE integration regression test reproducing an aliased, multi-refresh session and asserting both fixes hold end-to-end. - Update any existing test that hard-codes the OLD "<role>-<index>" id in report.json/report.md. OUT (do NOT touch): - archiveFetch (self-resolves alias; changing it risks the evidence-path join). - incrementRefreshCount body, monitor loop, PlaylistSelection.Candidate (already alias-correct). - SessionReportBuilder / PlaylistInfoFormatter (04 redesign — correct; the join was never the bug). - Rule engine, finding emission, alias scheme, master trackPlaylist call @:199. - sessionRefreshTotal semantics; Summary "Refreshes" stays max-across-playlists (04 contract).
task_brief.implementation_path:
Direction: UNIFY ON ALIAS (presentationID). Single source of truth for media playlist identity.
EDIT 1 — master-children loop (ValidationSession.swift ~230-240). Hoist presentationID ABOVE trackPlaylist and pass it as the track id: let playlistID = "\(reference.role.rawValue)-\(index)" let presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: reference.url)?.alias ?? playlistID await archiveFetch(load.result, requestURL: reference.url, playlistID: presentationID) if let playlist = load.playlist { trackPlaylist(presentationID, kind: .media, role: reference.role, url: reference.url, selected: true, refreshCount: 1) let loadedKind = playlist.media.map(classifier.classify) emitPlaylistInformation(playlistID: presentationID, playlist: playlist, streamKind: loadedKind) } (archiveFetch arg switched playlistID→presentationID for call-site clarity only; behavior identical since it self-resolves. The local `let presentationID` previously at ~234 is removed — now declared once above. Do not leave a duplicate declaration.)
EDIT 2 — direct-media else (ValidationSession.swift ~253-261). Same hoist: let presentationID = aliasRegistry.alias(for: inputURL)?.alias ?? "media" trackPlaylist(presentationID, kind: .media, role: .variant, url: inputURL, selected: true, refreshCount: 1) emitRoster(masterURL: nil, references: []) let loadedKind = rootPlaylist.media.map(classifier.classify) emitPlaylistInformation(playlistID: presentationID, playlist: rootPlaylist, streamKind: loadedKind) (Removes the old `let presentationID` previously at ~255.)
NO other source edits. incrementRefreshCount(presentationID) now hits the track → A fixed. PlaylistInfo.id == presentationID == PlaylistInformation.playlistID → infoByID join hits → B fixed.
task_brief.code_level_guidance:
ID FLOW — before vs after (media playlist "V0"):
BEFORE (split): trackPlaylist key ──"variant-0"──┐ ├─✗ mismatch info / increment / archive ─"1080p_avc1-mp4a"(alias) → PlaylistInfo.id="variant-0"; infoByID["1080p_avc1-mp4a"]; increment["1080p_avc1-mp4a"]→miss
AFTER (unified on alias): trackPlaylist key ─────────────┐ info / increment / archive ────┼─"1080p_avc1-mp4a" (single id) PlaylistInfo.id ───────────────┘ → infoByID hit (fields render) ; increment hit (count advances)
MASTER stays correct: its alias is "master" by scheme, equal to its track key @:199. Leave it. No-alias edge: init-loop fallback (playlistID) == monitor fallback (candidate.id) == "<role>-<index>", so keys still agree even when alias absent. No special-casing required.
EXPECTED OUTPUT CHANGE (intended, not a regression): - report.md: media blocks now show "- Type: …, - HLS version: …, …" + "### Timing"; subtitle and Summary show the real refresh count (max media refreshCount, e.g. 35); master subtitle refresh stays 1 (master is fetched once — correct). - report.json: each playlist object's id field changes from "<role>-<index>" to the alias. This is DESIRED (aligns json with md/archive/evidence). Any frozen-json snapshot test asserting "variant-0"/"audio-0"/… must be updated to the alias. This supersedes the 04 "json byte-identical" note (that constraint was scoped to the 04 render change, not to this id fix).
Summary "Refreshes" = playlists.map(\.refreshCount).max(). Master=1, media=real → max=real. Good.
task_brief.entry_points: Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/ValidationSession.swift (EDIT 1 + 2) Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/ValidationSession+Monitoring.swift (read — increment caller) Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/ValidationSession+Reporting.swift (read — PlaylistInfo build + archiveFetch self-resolve) Valistream/ValistreamCore/Sources/ValistreamCore/Session/PlaylistSelection.swift (read — Candidate.id) Valistream/Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/ (add regression test)
task_brief.search_fallback: grep "playlistTracks" for the dict + its 2 reads. grep "incrementRefreshCount" for the increment site (Monitoring) + def. grep "let presentationID" for the alias-derivation pattern to mirror. grep "<role>-<index>" intent via "rawValue)-\(index" for the id-construction line. For breaking tests: grep "variant-0"/"audio-0"/"subtitles-0" and "Refreshes:" across Tests dirs.
task_brief.acceptance_criteria: - Multi-refresh aliased live session: report.md Summary "Refreshes: N" with N > 1 (matches real snapshot count); per-media subtitle shows "N refresh[es]" with the real count; master shows 1. - Every media "## <glyph> <id>" block contains flat property bullets ("- Type:" … "- Protection:") and, for media, a "### Timing" subsection. No empty media blocks. - Master block unchanged (still renders its fields; no Timing). - report.json playlist ids equal the aliases (e.g. "1080p_avc1-mp4a"), consistent with report.md + archive folders; refreshCount fields hold the real per-playlist counts. - No new build warnings; no duplicate `presentationID` declarations left behind.
task_brief.validation: - Build scheme `Valistream` (xcode-tools BuildProject) → 0 errors. - Run `Valistream.xctestplan` (RunAllTests) → all pass. Update expectations where the OLD "<role>-<index>" id appears in report.json/md assertions. Likely touch points to CHECK (update only if they assert the old id; many already use aliases): Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/PlaylistIDSchemeTests.swift Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/PlaylistInfoBlockTests.swift Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/NormalSessionReadabilityTests.swift ValistreamCoreTests/Session/SessionReportTests.swift (json id field, if asserted) Renderer unit tests (ReportMarkdownTests/SessionReportTimelineInfoTests) build matching-id fixtures and should NOT need changes. - ADD regression test (unit-testing.md conventions), e.g. Valistream/ValistreamIntegrationTests/RefreshCountAndMediaFieldsTests.swift: * ScriptedStreamFetcher: master with ≥1 aliased variant; stub the variant media URL to return a CHANGING body across ≥2 refreshes (advance media-sequence so RefreshScheduler keeps polling). * Run a bounded session (deadline/timeLimit a few target-durations, nonInteractive). * Read report.md; assert: Summary "Refreshes:" value > 1; the variant block subtitle contains "refreshes"; the variant block contains "- Type:" AND "### Timing". * Guards BOTH bugs at the integration seam the renderer-unit tests structurally cannot reach (they control both sides of the id join with one literal). - Manual: regenerate a real session, confirm media blocks populated + refresh count realistic.
